data.title: Quantum Computing News: Rigetti Reports $216 Million Loss as Pasqal and Xanadu Target Billion Dollar Listings
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's update on quantum news. In early 2026, the story is still about progress, but it is also about money, and where large buyers may place bets. This round covers an EU draft shift, fresh results from Rigetti, two new SPAC paths for Pasqal and Xanadu, and a sharp AI proof moment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

1. EU Draft Shifts Away From Quantum

First, a leaked draft tied to the EU Industrial Accelerator Act may drop quantum, AI, and chips from the list of tech that must be made in the EU to get state funds and bids. Instead, the draft relies on heavy goods and clean power, such as steel, cement, EVs, solar, wind, battery packs, and hydro power. In turn, this may leave less direct lift for local quantum build plans, even as EU states still debate how far to push buy local rules.

2. Rigetti Posts Small Sales and Big Cash

Next, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) reported $7.1 million in 2025 sales and a $216.2 million net loss, while maintaining $589.8 million in cash and near cash. It also tied that cash to more build work, plus more on-site deals, led by an $8.4 million order from India's C DAC for a 108-qubit system due in the back half of 2026. In the same note, Chief Executive Subodh Kulkarni said, "In 2025, we made great progress across fidelity, scale, and system architecture," as the firm also flagged gains in gate scores and chiplet tests.

3. Pasqal Takes a SPAC Route to Nasdaq

Meanwhile, Pasqal said it will merge with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II (BBCQ) in a deal that aims to list the new firm on Nasdaq in the back half of 2026. The deal sets a value of about $2.0 billion and cites about $200 million in a new note fund, with a total cash pot of more than $600 million if key steps close. Also, Pasqal says it now runs seven live systems and works with more than 25 clients and allies as it seeks to scale and drive more paid use.

4. Xanadu Lines Up a Public Debut

Also, Xanadu said it plans to go public via Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. (CHAC), with a deal value of about $3.1 billion and an aim for a large cash pool post close if redemptions stay low. The pitch is its light-based route, plus a plan for linked and modular gear via its Aurora system, with more focus on net-like links than one huge chip. In that frame, Chief Exec Christian Weedbrook said, "As we move closer to becoming a publicly listed company, we recognize that our photonic approach to quantum computing represents a new and differentiated opportunity in the public markets."

5. AI Hits a Real Math Win

Finally, a side story may still matter for quantum, since it speaks to how fast tools can now aid hard proof work. A report said Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 solved a known graph task on a set of cases, after a prompt from Filip Stappers, with Don Knuth tied to the past work on the topic. In plain terms, it showed AI can do more than chat, since it can log steps, test code, and land on a rule set that holds for many runs, which may help speed up math work that feeds deep tech over time.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up IonQ (IONQ), Rigetti, D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), alongside other notable quantum stocks. It's a great tool for gaining an in-depth view of each pure-play quantum stock and the broader quantum computing industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: GM and Ford: Bank of America Names 2 Top Auto Stocks to Buy for 2026
data.description:
The changeover of Presidential Administrations always brings changes of policy priorities, and those can ripple outward. Sometimes, it's impossible to know where the ripples will end, and sometimes, it's easy. Last month, one of the easier ripples made itself felt.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The second Trump Administration took office promising to roll back large parts of the Biden regulatory regime, including difficult findings on greenhouse gases and automobile fuel economy. The greenhouse gas standard, the GHG, is a rollback, meaning that the Feds have rescinded their designation of carbon dioxide as a 'threat to public health and welfare.' The fuel economy measure concerns CAFE, or corporate average fuel economy, which is used to set gas mileage and efficiency standards for cars on the road. Trump's Administration has lowered the standard, making it easier for automakers to comply – the higher Biden standard was designed to promote construction of electric vehicles.

This new regulatory model will allow Detroit's major automakers, Ford and GM, to lean harder into high-margin trucks and SUVs, their big money-makers, while dialing back orders for less popular electric vehicles.

Bank of America is following this conclusion to its logical end: that the Detroit legacy names are going to benefit from this chance to change course under a more favorable regulatory environment. The bank is naming General Motors (NYSE:GM) and Ford (NYSE:F) – two legendary names in the automotive industry – as top auto stocks for 2026. Let's give them a closer look, and find out why these veterans of Detroit's Big Three are getting Bank of America's thumbs-up.

General Motors

We'll start with General Motors, the owner of the Chevy, Buick, Cadillac, and GMC nameplates. These include some highly recognizable names, such as the Chevy Silverado pickup truck and the Cadillac Escalade SUV. GM has been selling industry-leading cars for over a century; the company was formed as a holding company in 1908, and its first brand acquisition was Buick. The company today boasts a $71 billion market cap, and in 2025 claimed a market share of the US automotive industry in excess of 17%.

A look at some numbers will show how strong GM's position is. Last year, the company's total auto sales came to 2.85 million vehicles, a figure that was up 6% year-over-year; two of GM's full-size pickups, the Chevy Silverado and the GMC Sierra, led the American market for the sixth consecutive year, with combined sales of 940,000 units. GM is also doing well in the lower-end parts of the market, and last year sold almost 700,000 Buick and Chevy cars priced below $30,000.

It's a known fact that electric vehicle sales have been declining in recent months – the Financial Times reports that the world's auto industry has absorbed a $65 billion hit over the past year from paring back its EV production – but internal combustion engine vehicles are rising. GM, last month, announced a $46 million investment at an assembly plant in Ontario, Canada – a plant that builds Chevy Silverado trucks.

When we turn to the direct financial results, we find that GM finished 2025 with $45.3 billion in revenue for the fourth quarter, down 5% year-over-year. The fourth-quarter figure missed the forecast by $750 million. At the same time, earnings were sound – the non-GAAP EPS, at $2.51, was up some 30% year-over-year, and exceeded the forecast by 25 cents per share.

Looking ahead for this automaker, Bank of America's Alexander Perry sees a strong future. The industry expert writes, "We believe GM is a key beneficiary of recent regulatory changes including the removal of CAFÉ penalties, and GHG relief, which is enabling a mix shift to its most margin accretive trucks/SUVs and away from unprofitable EVs. The evidence is compelling: we estimate that trucks/SUVs variable profit per unit is $17.5K vs. corporate average of $10-12K. We also believe GM should benefit in 2026 from lower warranty costs & regulatory credits, lean inventory levels, and low incentives."

With these potential gains in mind, Perry rates GM as a Buy, with a $105 price target that implies a one-year upside of 34%. (To watch Perry's track record, click here.)

GM's shares are currently trading for $78.60; the average price target, at $95.76, suggests a gain of 22% for the year ahead. The stock's Moderate Buy consensus rating is based on 19 recent recommendations that include 14 Buys, 4 Holds, and 1 Sell. (See GM stock forecast.)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Chinese Chip Chiefs Call for a National Effort to Take On Europe and the U.S.
data.description:
Chinese semiconductor chiefs have called on its Government to launch a national effort to challenge the power of global rivals such as Dutch giant ASML (ASML) as well as ramp-up the AI race with the U.S.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Chinese Self-Resilience

Zhao Jinrong, chairman of Naura Technology Group, Chen Nanxiang, chairman and president of ‌Yangtze Memory Technologies Corp, and Liu Weiping, chairman of Empyrean Technology have grouped together to write an article urging an effort to develop operational lithography systems during the 2026 to 2030 economic five-year plan period.

They called on the government to pool national resources to integrate technological breakthroughs across institutions and boost their self-resilience.

"Taking lithography machines as an example, ASML's extreme ultraviolet (EUV) equipment has 100,000 components supplied by ‌5,000 suppliers, with ASML merely serving ⁠as the integrator," the executives wrote in Science and Technology Review. "How to establish China's ASML, so that the 'integrated' can jump out of the barrier of 'fame and fortune', and uniformly allocate ​funds ​and human resources is an urgent issue."

ASML Holding has become the world's sole ⁠supplier of EUV lithography machines, essential for producing the most advanced semiconductor chips used to power smartphones, AI and advanced computing.

The article also identified bottlenecks in electronic design automation (EDA) software, materials such as silicon wafers and electronic gases as key areas requiring national-level coordination.

5-Year AI Action Plan

The five-year roadmap, however, did not specifically mention lithography machines, calling ‌instead to 'improve advanced process manufacturing capabilities, accelerate the ​development of key equipment, materials ​and components."

The plan though did lay out its ambitions to ​aggressively adopt artificial intelligence throughout its economy and dominate emerging technologies such as quantum computing and humanoid robots.

The ‌country will "seize the commanding heights of science and technological development" and seek "decisive breakthroughs in key core technologies."

The 141-page blueprint mentioned AI more than 50 times and included an "AI+ action plan."

This includes experimenting with robots to perform jobs in sectors suffering from labor shortages and deploying AI agents that can perform tasks with minimal human guidance.

"Beijing's goal is to use AI and robotics to boost productivity and performance in a wide range ​of sectors, from manufacturing and logistics to education and healthcare," said Kyle Chan, fellow in Chinese technology at the Brookings Institution think tank.

What are the Best Chinese Stocks to Buy Now?

We have rounded up the best Chinese stocks to buy using our TipRanks comparison tool. As can be seen below, Baidu (BIDU) has the best upside with 52.27%.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Iran Conflict Sends Fertilizer Prices Higher, Raising Cost Risks for Tyson, Hormel, and Food Stocks
data.description:
A new risk is forming in the global food chain as conflict in Iran begins to disrupt fertilizer supply from the Middle East. The issue centers on the Strait of Hormuz, a key sea route used by ships carrying fertilizer to farms around the world.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Middle East is one of the largest fertilizer regions. At the same time, the strait is a vital shipping route. About 35% of global urea exports pass through this route, according to CRU data. Urea is the most widely used nitrogen fertilizer and supports nearly half of global food output.

Production Stops, and Prices Move Higher

First signs of stress in the market have already appeared. QatarEnergy halted output of sulphur, ammonia, and urea at its Ras Laffan complex after a drone strike hit the site. Qatar normally exports about 5.4 million tons of urea each year, close to 10% of global seaborne supply.

At the same time, Iran has taken all ammonia output offline as the conflict continues. Other producers in the region are now weighing output cuts as ships struggle to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

The price reaction has been quick. Granular urea prices in the Middle East have risen from about $130 to roughly $575 to $650 per ton in just days. Egyptian export prices have climbed from about $125 to around $610 to $625 per ton.

Ammonia futures have also surged. A European cargo for April recently traded at about $725 per ton, roughly $130 above its last trade in mid February.

Additionally, energy costs are adding more pressure. Natural gas is the key input used to make nitrogen fertilizer. Svein Tore Holsether, chief executive of Yara International, said gas used by the firm in Europe rose from $10.6 per mmbtu to more than $20 within a few days.

Holsether said the focus on oil markets may be hiding the scale of the fertilizer risk. "We shouldn't underestimate what this potentially could mean for global food production," he said.

He also warned about the impact on yields if supply remains tight. "If you're not getting fertilizer into the field of the farmers, yields could go down by up to 50% in the first harvest."

Food Stocks Could Feel the Impact

Unlike oil shocks, fertilizer supply issues tend to show up in food prices with a delay. Farmers apply fertilizer months before crops reach stores.

Raj Patel, a food system expert at the Lyndon B. Johnson School of Public Affairs, said bread prices could rise within six to ten weeks if supply remains tight. Egg prices may move higher within a few months, while pork and chicken prices could rise within six months.

This type of cost pressure can ripple across large food producers such as Tyson Foods (TSN), Hormel Foods (HRL), General Mills (GIS), and The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC).

Meanwhile, fertilizer producers like Nutrien Ltd. (NTR), The Mosaic Company (MOS), and CF Industries Holdings (CF) could see support from higher fertilizer prices if supply remains constrained.

Analysts say the disruption could prove more complex than the fertilizer shock that followed Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Chris Lawson, head of fertilizers at CRU, said the key difference now is the shipping route itself. "When prices spiked in 2022, it was extraordinary, but the market was able to adjust because Russian exports continued," he said.

If the Strait of Hormuz becomes harder to cross, fertilizer supply could face a more direct physical limit. In that case, the ripple effect across global food markets may only be starting.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all the stocks appearing in the piece. It's a great tool to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader food industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/5/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Wobble amid Rising U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description:
U.S. stock futures wobbled early Thursday as traders watched for signs of de-escalation amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.03%, 0.05%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 5:26 a.m. EST on March 5.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major averages rebounded sharply as investors' war-related anxiety eased. The S&P 500 closed up 0.78%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq added 1.29%, and the Dow rose 0.49%.

Technology and semiconductor names led the gains, with Nvidia (NVDA) up 1.7%, Broadcom (AVGO) gaining 1.2%, and Micron (MU), Intel (INTC), and AMD (AMD) each surging over 5%.

On the economic front, traders anticipate weekly jobless claims data.

Moreover, on the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Costco (COST), Marvell (MRVL), JD.com (JD), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.12%. WTI crude oil futures were up $76.39 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price traded around $5,161 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as investors tracked Middle East geopolitical developments.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets rose on March 5, tracking Wall Street gains.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.28%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.64%, while the Shenzhen Component increased 0.81%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei surged 1.90%, with the Topix up 1.90%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: "It May Not Have Been My Wisest Tweet": Elon Musk Defends Post at Center of Twitter Deal Lawsuit
data.description:
Elon Musk told a San Francisco jury this week that a key tweet during his 2022 bid to buy Twitter was not meant to move the stock price and was probably not his wisest tweet. The post is now at the center of a lawsuit from investors who claim the message caused them losses.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The dispute dates back to Musk's $44 billion deal to buy Twitter in April 2022, before the company changed its name to X. At the time, Musk agreed to pay $54.20 per share and signed a binding deal with the company's board.

Soon after that deal was signed, Musk began to question the number of spam and bot accounts on the site. Then, on May 13, he posted on Twitter that the deal was "temporarily on hold" until the firm could prove that fake accounts made up less than 5% of users.

The message quickly moved the market.

When trading opened that day, Twitter shares fell about 9%. Over the next few weeks, the stock continued to slide and later traded just above $30. Some investors sold their shares at those lower prices. However, the deal later closed at the full price of $54.20 per share. The investors who sold early say Musk's posts misled the market and caused them to miss out on the higher payout.

Musk Says Tweet Was Not Meant to Move Markets

In court, Musk said the tweet was not meant as a sign that the deal might fail. Instead, he told the jury he was simply raising concerns about bots on the platform.

"It may not have been my wisest tweet," Musk said during testimony. "I am not sure I would call it incredibly stupid… but if it led to this trial, it probably qualifies as such."

Musk also explained that the phrase "on hold" was meant in a basic way. He compared it to saying he might be late to a meeting rather than canceling it.

He also said he did not think about how traders would read the post before sending it. The tweet was written early in the morning, and he did not review it with advisers first.

Musk added that stock markets often react in ways that are hard to predict. He told the court that markets can behave like a "manic depressive," meaning sharp swings can occur even without clear reasons.

Investors Point to Banker Emails

Meanwhile, lawyers representing the investors say the tweet was part of a plan to gain leverage in the deal talks. They showed the jury emails from Barclays bankers sent to Musk before the tweet was posted.

One message suggested that raising questions about bots could create "an opportunity to revisit price." The banker added that the threat of walking away from the deal might push Twitter to accept a lower offer.

After Musk's tweet, another message from Barclays said investors now gave the deal "50/50 odds." The email also said the stock might stay low because traders feared more tweets that could affect the deal. Musk replied to that message with a short note that said, "Matches my understanding of things."

Still, Musk denied the claim that he tried to move the stock price. He said he was simply speaking his mind about issues with the platform. The jury will now decide whether the tweets crossed a legal line. If the investors win the case, damages could reach billions of dollars.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Trade Desk Stock (TTD) Pops on Potential OpenAI Deal
data.description:
Trade Desk (TTD) stock surged about 15% in Thursday's extended trading session after The Information, citing sources, reported that OpenAI held early talks with the company for a partnership to grow the AI startup's ad business. Trade Desk offers ad buyers a cloud-based platform to create and optimize their digital advertising campaigns across ad formats and devices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Potential Partnership Between Trade Desk and OpenAI

The Information noted that while OpenAI plans to develop in-house ad-tech functions eventually, the potential collaboration with Trade Desk suggests the AI firm might rely on external partners in the meantime to boost its ad business. OpenAI launched ads in ChatGPT in February 2026.

Advertising could prove vital for OpenAI, as the company aims to monetize its user base of 920 million. The AI startup expects advertising to help double its revenue from its consumer ChatGPT business to $17 billion in 2026. Also, ads could become more crucial for OpenAI as it has reportedly dropped its plans to roll out e-commerce directly within ChatGPT.

Meanwhile, a deal with OpenAI could help revive Trade Desk's business. TTD stock has plunged 34% so far in 2026 as investors are concerned about the company's uneven performance. Moreover, fears that new tools from Anthropic and OpenAI will impact traditional software companies have also weighed on investor sentiment.

TTD stock took a major hit in late February when the ad-tech platform announced a disappointing first-quarter outlook. Notably, the company's Q1 adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) guidance of about $195 million significantly lagged the Street's $223 million estimate.

Is TTD a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Trade Desk stock based on 16 Buys, 14 Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average TTD stock price target of $35.46 indicates 41% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Down Today, 3/5/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down ahead of Thursday's session as investors watched new military developments in the Middle East amid the expanding conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.33%, 0.40%, and 0.30%, respectively, at 2:47 a.m. EST on March 5.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the day, Wall Street had already posted solid gains. The rebound was driven by strength in big tech and semiconductor stocks. The Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq rose 0.5%, 0.8%, and 1.3%, respectively.

Leading the gains were semiconductor stocks. Nvidia (NVDA) shares rose more than 1%. Other chipmakers, including Broadcom (AVGO), Micron Technology (MU), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC), also posted gains. Meanwhile, consumer staples, energy, and materials were the only S&P 500 sectors that ended the day in negative territory.

Investors now turn their attention to several major earnings reports due tomorrow. Kroger (KR), Burlington (BURL), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ) report Thursday morning, while Costco (COST) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) release results after the close. Weekly jobless claims are also due Thursday, adding another data point to a busy market week.
